title: SEP Telemetry
description: Optional, opt-in engagement telemetry that a consumer can share with a provider after a session. Telemetry creates a value exchange: the provider receives signal quality data to improve future recommendations, and the consumer receives better-tailored payloads. Telemetry is never required and the consumer always controls what is shared.

sep_version.description: The version of the Stream Exchange Protocol this telemetry payload conforms to.

unit_interval.description: A normalized score between 0 and 1 inclusive.

telemetry_item.description: Engagement data for a single content item within the session. Captures viewing behavior signals that help the provider understand content quality and relevance.

item_id.description: The SEP item_id of the content item this telemetry refers to.

viewed.description: Whether the consumer viewed (started playback of) this item.

view_duration_seconds.description: How many seconds of the content the consumer watched.

completed.description: Whether the consumer watched or consumed the item to completion.

liked.description: Whether the consumer explicitly liked or favorited the item.

skipped_at_seconds.description: The timestamp in seconds at which the consumer skipped to the next item. Only present if the item was skipped.

rewatched.description: Whether the consumer replayed or re-consumed the item during the session.

paused.description: Whether the consumer paused during playback.

paused_at_seconds.description: The timestamp in seconds at which the consumer first paused. Only meaningful if paused is true.

telemetry.description: The telemetry payload containing session-level and item-level engagement data.

session_id.description: A unique identifier for the session this telemetry covers.

items.description: Per-item engagement data for each content item the consumer interacted with during the session.

session_completed.description: Whether the consumer completed the full session arc or exited early.

session_satisfaction.description: The consumer's self-reported satisfaction with the session, from 0 (poor) to 1 (excellent). May be inferred from behavior rather than explicitly asked.
